litigation.mdlName: In re: Glucagon-like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists (GLP-1 RAs) Products Liability Litigation
litigation.court: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Pennsylvania

phaseDetail: Under Case Management Order No. 32 (April 30, 2026), expert-admissibility and summary judgment briefing runs through August 7, 2026, with Rule 702 hearings set for September 10-18, 2026. Bellwether trial dates have not yet been set and are expected to follow those rulings. NAION vision-loss claims proceed separately in MDL-3163.

pendingCounts.scope: Pending actions in separate NAION vision-loss MDL (MDL-3163)

settlements.label: No settlements announced
settlements.detail: No global or individual settlement program has been announced. The Rule 702 and summary judgment rulings expected after the September 2026 hearings are the litigation's next major value-shaping events. Industry estimates of potential exposure are not promises: no outcome is guaranteed and defendants continue to dispute the claims.

keyDates.label: MDL-3094 created
keyDates.detail: JPML centralized federal GLP-1 cases before Judge Karen Spencer Marston in the Eastern District of Pennsylvania.

keyDates.label: CMO No. 32 sets expert and summary judgment schedule
keyDates.detail: Fifth amended scheduling order for cross-cutting issues: briefing through August 7, 2026.

keyDates.label: 3,763 pending federal cases
keyDates.detail: MDL continued steady monthly growth through the first half of 2026 (JPML).

keyDates.label: Rule 702 (Daubert) hearings: September 10-18, 2026
keyDates.detail: Hearings on expert-admissibility motions per CMO No. 32. Bellwether trial dates have not yet been set and are expected to follow these rulings.
